summary: Keep packaged Windows Sandbox automation bound to the focused workbench editor when the M6 model-history rail also mounts read-only CodeMirror comparison panes.

decisions.decision: Define one packaged-workbench editor selector and use it for editor discovery, source reads, replacements, and appends instead of selecting the first global CodeMirror content node.
decisions.rationale: The retained exact-head Sandbox screenshot and runner log prove the M4 walkthrough left History open with two read-only CodeMirror panes before N-2 began. The global selector chose Current source and WebDriver rejected the first cycle as non-interactable, while the focused main editor remained visible and healthy. Scoping to the focused editor group preserves the intended black-box interaction and prevents History or future comparison views from intercepting edits.

testEvidence.criterion: The packaged reliability harness must edit the focused workbench document even when History comparison editors are mounted first in DOM order.
testEvidence.test: Reproduce the three-CodeMirror structure in a DOM fixture and require the shared selector to resolve the focused workbench editor.

red.outcome: RED: the new regression test failed while 56 existing tests passed because no focused-workbench selector existed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 57 packaged-evidence helper tests passed; the fixture contained three .cm-content nodes and the selector resolved only the focused workbench editor.

testEvidence.criterion: A failed release soak attempt must be diagnosed from retained evidence before any rerun.
testEvidence.test: Inspect the exact-head network-disabled Sandbox runner log, screenshot, and source metadata after the first attempted literal run.

red.outcome: RED before the timed soak began: the clean build and full pre-soak cube/export/MCP walkthrough passed, then the first N-2 edit failed with element not interactable. Retained screenshot 02-mcp-pending-diff.png showed History open with two read-only comparison editors before the real focused editor, proving selector interception rather than a product edit failure.

green.command: Source inspection plus focused-selector regression test; runtime requalification follows only from a clean corrected commit.
green.outcome: GREEN for the targeted correction: every packaged editor read/write path now uses the shared focused-workbench selector and no generic find(".cm-content") remains. No claim is made that N-2 passed in the failed run.

questions: Q-0042 remains the only literal M6 specification contradiction and is unaffected by this release-harness correction.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
